paper_metadata.title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
paper_metadata.abstract: Transformers-based models, such as BERT, have been one of the most successful deep learning models for NLP. Unfortunately, one of their core limitations is the quadratic dependency (mainly in terms of memory) on the sequence length due to their full attention mechanism. To remedy this, we propose, BigBird, a sparse attention mechanism that reduces this quadratic dependency to linear. We show that BigBird is a universal approximator of sequence functions and is Turing complete, thereby preserving these properties of the quadratic, full attention model. Along the way, our theoretical analysis reveals some of the benefits of having $O(1)$ global tokens (such as CLS), that attend to the entire sequence as part of the sparse attention mechanism. The proposed sparse attention can handle sequences of length up to 8x of what was previously possible using similar hardware. As a consequence of the capability to handle longer context, BigBird drastically improves performance on various NLP tasks such as question answering and summarization. We also propose novel applications to genomics data.

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD introduces a sparse attention mechanism that reduces the quadratic dependency on sequence length to linear, allowing for longer context handling and improved performance on various NLP tasks.
extraction_data.research_problem: Limited context handling in Transformers due to quadratic dependency on sequence length
extraction_data.application: Question answering, natural language understanding, and text classification
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD introduces a sparse attention mechanism that allows for longer input sequences, enabling the model to capture more context and improve performance on natural language processing tasks.
extraction_data.extension: The BIGBIRD model uses a sparse attention mechanism to reduce the computational complexity of self-attention.
extraction_data.research_problem: Modeling longer input sequences for natural language processing tasks
extraction_data.application: Natural language processing, question answering, masked language modeling
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD introduces a sparse attention mechanism that allows for longer input sequences, enabling the model to capture more context and improve performance on natural language processing tasks.
extraction_data.extension: The BIGBIRD model uses a sparse attention mechanism to reduce the computational complexity of self-attention.
extraction_data.research_problem: Modeling longer input sequences for natural language processing tasks
extraction_data.application: Natural language processing, question answering, masked language modeling
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces a new Transformer architecture that can handle longer sequences by using a combination of local and global attention mechanisms.
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD introduces a sparse attention mechanism that allows for longer sequence lengths while maintaining performance competitive with full attention models.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, question answering, text generation
extraction_data.research_problem: Long-range dependency modeling in natural language processing
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Model (MLM), Next Sentence Prediction (NSP)
